library.description: ملحق SDL3 الرسمي للصوت والمزج وتشغيل الملفات والمسارات الصوتية.

kind.description: أنواع مجردة أو صريحة تحمل معنى دلالياً وتظهر في التواقيع والبنى.

identity.name: MIX_GroupMixCallback
identity.titleArabic:
A callback that fires when a MIX_Group
has completed mixing

summary.actualTranslationArabic:
A callback that fires when a MIX_Group
has completed mixing
summary.actualMeaningArabic: يمثل هذا النوع نوع MIX Group Mix Callback، أي القيمة أو المقبض أو المؤشر الذي تتعامل معه بقية الواجهة في هذا السياق.
summary.purposeArabic: يستخدم هذا النوع عندما تحتاج القيمة أو المقبض أو المؤشر الذي يمثله داخل بقية الواجهة.
summary.whyUseArabic: القيمة الحقيقية هنا أنه يمثل نوع القيمة التي تتداولها بقية الواجهة بين الحقول والدوال.
summary.carriedValueArabic: يحمل هذا النوع القيمة الفعلية أو المقبض أو المؤشر الذي تمرره بقية الدوال والحقول في هذا المسار.
summary.sourceDescription:
A callback that fires when a MIX_Group
has completed mixing.

signature.raw: typedef void (SDLCALL *MIX_GroupMixCallback)(void *userdata, MIX_Group *group, const SDL_AudioSpec *spec, float *pcm, int samples);

remarks:
This callback is fired when a mixing group has finished mixing: all
tracks in the group have mixed into a single buffer and are prepared to
be mixed into all other groups for the final mix output.

The audio data passed through here is not const data; the
app is permitted to change it in any way it likes, and those changes
will propagate through the mixing pipeline.

An audiospec is provided. Different groups might be in different
formats, and an app needs to be able to handle that, but SDL_mixer
always does its mixing work in 32-bit float samples, even if the inputs
or final output are not floating point. As such,
 spec->format will always be SDL_AUDIO_F32
and pcm hardcoded to be a float pointer.

samples is the number of float values pointed to by
 pcm : samples, not sample frames! There are no promises how
many samples will be provided per-callback, and this number can vary
wildly from call to call, depending on many factors.
